subject: Public Health
title: Food Hygiene and Safety
description: A 60-second lesson on Food Hygiene and Safety.

hook.text: Unsafe food causes 600 million illnesses and 420,000 deaths every year—a hidden epidemic.

buildup.text: Bacteria like Salmonella, E. coli, and Listeria contaminate food through poor handling, storage, and cooking.

discovery.text: The WHO's five keys: keep clean, separate raw and cooked, cook thoroughly, keep safe temperatures, use safe water.

twist.text: Street food—eaten by 2.5 billion people daily—is both a nutrition lifeline and a food safety challenge.

climax.text: Investing in food safety infrastructure in low-income countries could prevent millions of illnesses annually.

punchline.text: The food that nourishes you can also poison you—handling makes the difference.

quiz.question: How many illnesses does unsafe food cause annually?
